data.context: first live interview: planning the heldout-suite artifact
defects: no self-contained briefing before question 1 (opened on a bare codename)
undefined shorthand throughout: v1, #5, seal, vault
options lacked explicit pros/cons/tradeoffs per choice
data.fix: SKILL.md briefing section + cold-reader standalone-readability rule + tradeoffs requirement; design D8 cold-reader bullet; synced to spec-interview-test
data.operator_verdict: coverage right; presentation overloaded the operator
data.principle: operators have small working contexts; they decide only as well as they can follow

data.caught: the plan recorded hashes that no verb ever recomputed (verify-at-consumption missing), and claimed tamper-evidence without an out-of-band anchor
data.question: if the test-author escaped restrictions and modified a test, what stops it updating the seal to match?
data.resolution: verify verb (divergence check before every suite run) + seal emits manifest sha256 for ledger/git anchoring + author-never-runs-seal role line + signing named as deferred escalation (non_goal)
data.skill_lesson: rule 4 now asks the tamper question for adversarial-value artifacts

data.defect_4: euphemistic cost-concealment: friction / a-door-is-walked-through hid that all automated progress stops pending manual operator intervention
data.fix: SKILL.md ground rule 7 (name costs plainly, lead with consequence, then justify); D8 cold-reader rule extended; plan decision 4 rewritten with COST STATED PLAINLY + the three-part justification
data.principle: a plainly-named cost earns operator scrutiny; euphemism is over-reliance manufacturing

data.defect: text density + compound coinage: the live launcher probe undefined; option A sub-points crammed multiple ideas per sentence — operator could not parse A(1)/A(2)
data.fix: SKILL.md briefing section density rule; question rewritten plainly and answered on the rewrite
data.principle: one idea per sentence; a compound jargon noun is multiple undefined terms; concrete walk-throughs beat abstract enumerations

data.lesson_2: the omission prompt caught a NAMED near-term requirement (Codex CLI soon after end-to-end confidence) that reshaped the launcher contract: tool-neutral params (worker triple + isolation INTENT block), fail-closed translation clause, per-launcher smoke probes
data.skill_fix: rule 1 now asks for the concreteness of named futures (which specific thing, how soon)

data.lesson_2_refined: operator introspection: the vague desire (other headless AI tools) had a specific instance (codex) in mind all along — the rule is not ask-for-concreteness but PUSH for the concrete next step: make the operator name the single highest-priority concrete addition and how soon

data.fix: claude_p.py --permission-mode bypassPermissions (sandbox is the wall, probed); re-run pending
data.vendor_break: acceptEdits leaves mutating Bash (git add/commit, python3) behind approval prompts headless workers cannot answer; worker completed the change but could not commit
data.wall_probe: DENIED — sandbox blocked ls/cat of the held-out workspace THROUGH BASH (the decision-4 bash gap is covered on this build when sandbox is on)
worked_live: author leg end-to-end (real Opus suite sealed under fails-on-base)

data.decision_5_cost_observed: attempt 2 repeated the failure blind on counts-only feedback, as ratified
data.thesis_event: the held-out suite CAUGHT the isolation regression: visible checks passed (file exists), the held-out test asserted content DENIED and failed the merge — P3v2-5 pattern live, run 2 of ever
data.wall_probe: READABLE — under bypassPermissions the worker could ls/cat the held-out workspace through bash; the sandbox confinement observed in run 1 is tied to the permission layer; the generated sandbox settings block alone does nothing (schema unverified/ignored)
worked_live: full cycle: author 8-test suite sealed (fob errored:4 failed:3 passed:1), gate-a1 51s, escalation, gate-a2, halt-all

data.confidence: doc-grounded (code.claude.com sandboxing+settings); run 3 arbitrates commit-under-auto-allow + denyRead-blocks-bash on 2.1.205
data.fix: wall moved to OS layer sandbox.filesystem.denyRead (Seatbelt/bubblewrap, mode-independent); sandbox.autoAllowBashIfSandboxed=true runs sandboxed bash unattended (no prompt/abort); allowUnsandboxedCommands=false closes the escape hatch; permission mode back to acceptEdits (covers edit tool); permissions.deny Read uses //abs absolute prefix (single / was project-relative footgun)
data.network_gap: sandbox network is allowlist-based; network=true means sandbox-default for now, external-allow is a separate probe
data.root_cause: two mistakes: (1) sandbox.network must be an object not a boolean (invented shape, ignored); (2) the read wall lived on the permission layer (permissions.deny Read) which bypassPermissions changes, so bypass dropped it

data.consequence: GEN/HARD correctness keep A3; FIX correctness demoted to single-source run-day observation; headline is 24/24 re-verified, not 36/36
data.defect: grade.py hardcoded GEN total=17 vs 16-test hidden suite (solved unreachable); results glob wrong layout (0 runs graded); latent AttributeError on result3_hard_* files

regrade.fix: not re-gradeable: ws_fix_* workspaces never committed

data.caveat: docs do not name the headless -p path explicitly (follows from --settings precedence); vendor-build — smoke run 3 arbitrates
data.defect: generated sandbox settings omitted failIfUnavailable; vendor default on sandbox-start failure (missing deps/unsupported platform) is WARN AND RUN UNSANDBOXED — silently voids the OS read wall while all visible checks pass, contradicting CONTRACT.md fail-closed clause
data.fix: sandbox.failIfUnavailable=true in claude_p.py build_settings; asserted in test_exec_loop; SMOKE.md + README threat model updated
data.found_by: operator, 2026-07-12; verified against code.claude.com/docs sandboxing + settings pages (claude-code-guide agent)

D4 harm magnitude -> Provisional (default stays Decided)
D11 layered-wall requirement stated; mechanisms vendor-build
D14/section-5 deletion clause requires non-inferiority at pre-registered margin
grading_method: warrant-vs-sufficiency contradiction resolved (any one letter = provenance; decay in-date; fit checks at use time)
absence rule: unoccupied-niche only, never cheap/valuable
distilled rows name source doc+section going forward (rule 6)
data.internal_md: P3v2-5 thesis n=2 existence (smoke run 2 added); comparative-half artifact bound stated
data.scope: overstated-language + methodology + design-status corrections from the independent docs/ review

data.failIfUnavailable: accepted by live CLI; worker ran normally with it in generated settings
data.notes: launched by session on explicit operator instruction; ran the stale standalone 2.1.202 binary — the version skew found here led to the single-updater shim (same-day fix)
data.simultaneous: commit ran unattended under sandbox auto-allow AND OS wall denied held-out read — the combination runs 1-2 each missed half of

data.decay: vendor-build — the shim glob depends on the app layout observed 2026-07-12
data.fix_harness: claude_p.py records binary path (+version on real runs) in every result.json (CONTRACT.md binary key); dry-run records path only (dry-run executes nothing)
data.fix_host: ~/.local/bin/claude replaced with a shim that execs the newest app-managed CLI — single updater, cannot diverge; fails loudly (exit 127) on layout change; standalone store left at ~/.local/share/claude for rollback
data.problem: two independent claude installs with independent updaters: desktop app managed CLI (~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude-code/<ver>, 2.1.205, app-updated) vs standalone (~/.local/bin/claude -> ~/.local/share/claude/versions/2.1.202, stale since Jul 6 — only self-updates when run, and the operator always uses the app)

one question per message with pros/cons/tradeoffs incl. recommendation costs
grounded in repo before asking (storage/id/order never asked)
unpinned edges written into the plan so the blind test-author knows what NOT to test
integration-task question asked explicitly (answer: carried by task 2)
data.purpose: payload for the v2 pipeline first full end-to-end composition run (interview -> preflight -> loop -> suites -> gate -> ledger), operator-kicked next window
data.questions: 6 shaping + omission prompt (feature choice, storage contract, normalization, filter arity, display, task split/integration)
data.t1_prep: T1_MODEL pin added to cache-weight runner same branch (fail-closed, no silent default)

data.behavior_verified: live CLI matches every ratified decision: normalization to [work,home] from mixed-case/padded/duplicate input, untagged renders byte-identical, case-insensitive filter, no-match empty exit 0, empty tag error exit 1 writes nothing; visible tests OK
data.outcome: green — first full composition run: interview-produced ratified plan through preflight, loop, blind suites, gates, merges, ledger
data.plan: spec-interview-test tags plan (f15be05), 2 tasks, 1 dependency edge
data.significance: design section 5 stage 2 complete; T11 integration probe #2; the full-end-to-end-confidence gate for the Codex CLI launcher is open

suites.list-tag-filter: 1 file, 12 tests, 8 fail-on-base / 4 guards — validated against the tree WITH task 1 merged (dependency ordering live)

data.warts: test-repo bootstrap had __pycache__ tracked; workers faithfully committed bytecode — fixed 08a8d0c; not a pipeline defect
data.workers: 4 sessions, all ok, all claude 2.1.205 (per-spawn binary provenance live, first outing)

data.contract: CONTRACT.md documents usage key (recommended, best-effort, never fails a good launch)
data.decay: vendor-build (json schema validated on 2.1.201 benchmark JSONs, re-probe each build)
data.launcher: claude_p.py runs --output-format json; parse_session (fail-safe) writes usage {input/output/cache_read/cache_creation tokens, cost_usd, num_turns, api_duration_ms} to result.json; stderr split from stdout; is_error folded into ok (fail-closed)
data.loop: seal record gains author_usage + suite {files,lines}; gate record gains impl_usage + churn {files,insertions,deletions} from git diff --shortstat main...branch
data.re_smoke: REQUIRED before reliance — touches vendor invocation + stdout shape; SMOKE.md run 4 check: usage.output_tokens and usage.cost_usd non-null
data.tests: 29 exec-loop tests green incl. 4 parse_session unit tests + churn/suite assertions in happy-path
data.trigger: e2e-run-1 could not answer per-worker token spend from harness artifacts (recovered forensically from Claude Code session logs); operator asked to capture spend + code produced durably

data.account_window_readout: only documented machine-readable source is statusline JSON rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage / seven_day.used_percentage (+resets_at), from most recent API response = server account-wide view
data.constraints: statusline runs only in interactive TERMINAL claude (not headless -p, not desktop app per P2-8); populates after 1 API round-trip; used_percentage coarse 0-100
data.finding: /usage exists (aliases /cost /stats), shows session cost + plan-window bars + per-skill/subagent/plugin breakdown, but is INTERACTIVE-PANEL-ONLY (not in headless supported-command list) and a LOCAL-machine estimate (excludes other devices/claude.ai)
data.launcher_validation: per-worker capture field paths (.usage.*_tokens snake_case, .total_cost_usd, .num_turns, .duration_api_ms) confirmed current vs code.claude.com/docs agent-sdk; run-level aggregates summed across turns = correct granularity; stream-json carries per-turn usage if ever needed
data.t1_change: protocol readout step now primary=statusline-dump recipe (jq append to ~/t1-rl.log) with mandatory pre-window dry-run test; fallback=manual /usage bar in app; both record value+timestamp

data.found_by: independent review of loop.py, 2026-07-12; all three claims verified against code before fixing
data.gap_1_closure: loop exited 0 after per-task merges; task N+1 could regress task N invisibly (per-task gates run only the current task verifiers) — D3 completion-granted-not-claimed gap. Fix: closure() re-runs every task check + every sealed suite against final main via merge-gate (--base main --ref main), stamped with plan-snapshot sha256 + main SHA, ledger outcome, exit 0 only behind it, idempotent on exact main+plan match, closure-failed blocker otherwise. Non-adaptive: results reach only the operator (D2 budget untouched).
data.gap_2_lock: flock was plan-workdir-scoped; two plan names (or two heldout dirs) targeting one repo took disjoint locks — D4 single-writer was per-plan. Fix: acquire_repo_lock via flock in git-common-dir (covers every checkout/worktree, outside worker diffs), kept alongside the workdir lock (which still protects shared bundles/reports).
data.gap_3_toctou: verify-then-merge race: main could move between gate verify and git merge, landing an unjudged tree. Fix: land with git merge --ff-only <report source sha> — the judged tree lands byte-identically or nothing does (merge-not-fast-forward blocker). Simpler than reviewer-proposed gate-materialized CAS; non-FF landing stays a named upgrade if the blocker ever fires.
data.tests: 34 green: cross-task regression caught by closure; closure stamp bound to main+plan; restart idempotency; repo-lock contention across plan names; external mid-run commit refused non-FF with nothing landed

data.finding: gate runs checks in a --no-ff --no-commit staged-merge worktree, so HEAD lags the judged tree and status is dirty BY CONSTRUCTION; the author wrote legitimate spec tests consulting git state (test_spec_artifacts_have_no_uncommitted_changes, test_committed_greeter_defines_farewell via HEAD) and they fail environmentally, deterministically, on every attempt
data.fix_proposed: gate commits the merge in its throwaway worktree before running checks (judged state == post-merge commit state; normalizes with closure env) + ROLE.md line on suite execution environment
data.outcome: red — and the red is a FINDING: gate execution-environment defect, not a vendor break and not a worker defect
data.prediction_preregistered: after gate-a1, before gate-a2: deterministic red, attempts-exhausted, same 2 tests — CONFIRMED (a2 failed identical test names)

release_checks_all_passed.redaction: counts-only held-out feedback confirmed in blocker last_feedback
release_checks_all_passed.telemetry: usage non-null + credible on all 3 workers: author $1.98/24576out/40turns, impl-a1 $0.58/4183out, impl-a2 $0.84/7756out; binary 2.1.207 recorded per spawn; churn {2 files,+4} matches diffs

data.scratch: mktemp dir, disposable; essentials quoted here
data.sequence: author sealed 2 files/286 lines (24 tests) -> impl-a1 visible checks pass, hidden 2/24 fail -> Opus escalation -> same 2 fail -> attempts-exhausted, exit 1

data.blindness_note: found BECAUSE the author was blind: a sighted author would have accommodated the staged merge and buried the inconsistency
data.contract: ROLE.md now states the suite execution environment: HEAD == judged tree, clean status; content assertions (incl. git show HEAD:) are fair game, history-shape assertions (messages/authorship/parents) are not — the merge commit is synthetic
data.defect: gate ran checks in a --no-ff --no-commit STAGED-merge worktree: HEAD lagged the judged tree and git status was dirty by construction, so legitimate author tests consulting git state failed environmentally on every attempt (found by smoke run 4: 2 tests, deterministic, both attempts, $3.40)
data.fix: gate commits the judged merge in its throwaway worktree before running checks (synthetic identity, --no-verify so target-repo hooks never run, fail-closed on commit error, judged_commit sha recorded in report); up-to-date/self-merge path (closure) was already clean and is unchanged
data.tests: gate regression (author-style git-state checks pass; self-merge path equally clean) + full exec-loop/heldout suites green

data.amendment_rule: any post-registration change = dated ledger amendment; results without amendment list are invalid
data.fixed_before_running: hypotheses H1-H3, arms, task composition, oracle design, outcomes, small-n decision rules incl. the pre-named R2-pressure consequence (default-on scope demotes to risk-tiered)
data.question: R2 null comparison first: full harness vs one strong plain session, arbiter-suite oracle + blinded review + seeded canaries; secrecy-vs-gating decomposition deferred to pilot 2
data.state: registered; scheduling blocks on operator naming target repos + reserving windows; est -70 over 8 tasks
